# Evolutionary Optimization

## The Physics

Effective organizations evolve through cycles of variation, selection, and
retention — not through periodic grand redesigns. Small experiments produce
variations. Successful variations are selected and retained. Failed variations
are discarded. Over time, the organization adapts to its environment.

This mirrors biological evolution: no designer needed, just a selection mechanism
and enough variation to explore the solution space.

## Why It Matters

- **Risk reduction**: Small experiments fail cheaply. Grand redesigns fail catastrophically.
- **Continuous improvement**: Evolution never stops. Redesign happens in expensive bursts.
- **Adaptation**: Evolutionary systems track environmental changes. Designed systems become stale.
- **Knowledge preservation**: Evolution retains what works. Redesign risks discarding proven patterns.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Evolutionary economics (Nelson & Winter): organizational routines as genes.
- Lean Startup (Ries): build-measure-learn as organizational evolution.
- Toyota Kata (Rother): improvement through iterative experimentation toward target conditions.
- Kaizen: continuous incremental improvement.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P08 (Audit and Learn) — the observation/measurement mechanism that enables selection
- JOS-P09 (Start Somewhere, Iterate) — the principle of iterative improvement
- JOS-S05 (Governance Object Lifecycle) — lifecycle stages ARE evolutionary states
